chapters.summary: The latest episode of the Indie Hackers podcast features an episode on cryptocurrencies. Courtland is convinced that nfts could be the future of the web. But nothing contained in this episode is financial advice. Do your own research.
chapters.gist: Indie Hackers: Cryptocurrency Episode 1
chapters.headline: Courtland is doing an episode on cryptocurrencies, specifically nfts

chapters.summary: Mohammad Iqbal and Heaton Shah talk about how to make money trading nfts. They say start with level one and buy coins on a Monday. You can make money, but you can also lose a lot of money.
chapters.gist: How to Make Money in Crypto With NFT
chapters.headline: Heat and Shah discuss how to make money trading nfts

chapters.summary: Nfts lie on the bedrock of a lot more complex tokens and crypto and blockchains. It can be hard to see why they're even new or useful compared to technology we already have on the Internet. It's hard to understand how to make actual use of them in a business.
chapters.gist: In the Elevator With Nfts and Crypto
chapters.headline: Nfts can be tricky to understand because they lie on the bedrock of many other cryptocurrencies

chapters.summary: Web three is the evolution of how we host and we do infrastructure kind of online. Now it's just kind of hosted in the cloud, but it's not even a cloud that we own or rent. So that fundamentally is kind of what has me interested and why I think it's the future.
chapters.gist: Top Executives: Web 3 Is the Future
chapters.headline: Was there ever a time where you were skeptics about nfts

chapters.summary: Nfts is a decentralized ledger, or you can think of it as a decentralized database. Instead of existing on one computer somewhere on the Internet, it exists across hundreds or thousands of millions of computers. This is like startups on steroids because the speed and pace is incredible.
chapters.gist: Are You Skeptical About Nfts?
chapters.headline: The blockchain is a decentralized ledger that exists across hundreds or thousands of computers

chapters.summary: NFT is a non fungible token. It's essentially digital property. Non fungible means each individual in one is its own distinct thing. The killer use case for this right now is people creating digital items.
chapters.gist: Non-Flatible Tokens Explained
chapters.headline: Think about fungible tokens. Bitcoin and ethereum are fungible

chapters.summary: Digital property has huge implications to how e commerce works. NFTs enable us to open up the world a little bit where if I build a game, I can now use anybody's NFT inside of my own platform. Will indie games become triple a hated games at some point?
chapters.gist: Will NFTs Open Up the Digital Property Market?
chapters.headline: I'm wondering what you guys think about this vision of digital property

chapters.summary: Courtland: Where do you think things are going in the future? Heaton: NFTs and what we're doing today is a killer use case. It has to do with ownership. Without the blockchain, that's not this. None of this would matter.
chapters.gist: Are NFTs a Shill Space?
chapters.headline: Courtland: Blockchain is a killer use case for NFTs

chapters.summary: It's not just about the content, it's about the community that you've built on that platform. I don't necessarily see a world in which people abandon Twitter or current social networks en masse because they don't own their stuff. I can see a path where people, bottom up, start using new apps and new websites.
chapters.gist: Trump on the Future of Social Networks
chapters.headline: I don't necessarily see a world in which people abandon Twitter or current social networks

chapters.summary: In 1995, a computer expert argued against predictions of what the Internet would do. He was wrong on every count. Even if you're very savvy, these futuristic predictions might look inaccurate at the time.
chapters.gist: Coming soon: The Internet's Predictions
chapters.headline: A Newsweek article from 1995 argued against predictions of what the Internet would do

chapters.summary: The ownership of digital assets in the video game industry is an absolute certainty in my mind. People are already buying sports collectibles as nfts already. How do you know that people will keep collecting things? Ultimately depends on what they're buying.
chapters.gist: Future of Digital Property: The Future of Collectibles
chapters.headline: What do you see digital property enabling in the future that might seem crazy today

chapters.summary: Mark Cuban is a big crypto junkie. NFT projects are in the Facebook apps phase, right? It might all disappear. Some of the use cases are sort of unimaginable.
chapters.gist: Keaton: Can NFT Projects Predict the Future?
chapters.headline: Keaton: I don't like to make predictions about the future

chapters.summary: Mubs: Turning digital items into NFTs and storing them on the blockchain for your users unlocks a lot of functionality. The items you put on the ledger are portable because the blockchain is publicly accessible. Mubs: From a creator economy, from a maker hack, indie hacker standpoint is something that is really interesting.
chapters.gist: The Blockchain and Creativity
chapters.headline: Mubs: Putting digital items on the blockchain unlocks a whole bunch of possibilities

chapters.summary: Chris Dixon: The marketing they do is just not traditional marketing. It's community marketing. The only time I saw such a tight feedback loop, whether it's NFTs or whatever, was clubhouse. The secondary system here on the blockchain that I think is going to flip a lot more stuff upside down.
chapters.gist: Chris Dixon On The Crypto Community
chapters.headline: Chris Dixon says cryptocurrency marketing is completely different from traditional marketing

chapters.summary: "It's changing who owns it. It's not some venture capital in Silicon Valley or LA or something who kind of owns the sort of rights to this thing, " he says. "All the video games that we play are now going to be controlled by the people actually play them "
chapters.gist: The Fight for the Future of Video Games
chapters.headline: The future of video games is changing who owns it

chapters.summary: Is there any connection between this fanciful world of NFTs and an actual business you're running as a founder? I'm interested in the whole DeFi space because it changes. At some point the model will flip. And so I need to know how that flip might happen.
chapters.gist: How to Invest in the Crypto World With a SaaS Company
chapters.headline: Let's talk about how you can actually invest in this as a founder today

chapters.summary: I would love to see a world where almost everything is represented as an NFT. That tells me something about the identity of the people who come to my website. It takes time to kind of, because those are old, established industries and technologies. But I think we'll kind of hit there at some point.
chapters.gist: What I Want to See on Founder Path and IndieHack
chapters.headline: I would love to see almost everything represented as an NFT

chapters.summary: Heaton: We're working on, I believe, basically, I think, redundancy for file storage. What if someone could access a document with their kind of metamask and their crypto credentials instead of their email? If you have something good that anyone, any SaaS business can do.
chapters.gist: Google's File Storage Security
chapters.headline: Heaton is working on security for file storage on the blockchain

chapters.summary: Simple idea with the community is people can tip each other with crypto somehow. Is does someone already have a wallet or not? Do they have they already jumped through those hoops? And if they haven't, is it your job to train them?
chapters.gist: Should have a community crypto tips?
chapters.headline: Simple idea with community is people can tip each other with crypto somehow

chapters.summary: Cortlandt: Buy crypto, but what next? Is there a particular website they should trust? Find a buddy. If you're really doing it for savings long term, don't put a lot of money in there. Follow trusted sources on Twitter.
chapters.gist: Buy Crypto With a Trustful Friend
chapters.headline: Cortlandt: You've said buy crypto, but what next

chapters.summary: All right, heat and mubs, thanks so much for coming on. Can you let listeners know where they can go to find out more about what you're up to with your SaaS businesses. You can find me on Twitter and all the usual social media places.
chapters.gist: Blair and Shashah on FounderPath
chapters.headline: Heat and mubs talk about their SaaS businesses

episode_title: #235 – An NFT Conversation for Indie Hackers with Hiten Shah and Mubashar Iqbal
